2606.22179.title: The Score Granularity Gap in Black-Box LLM Classification: A Comparative Study of Confidence Constructions
2606.22179.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as black-box classifiers in pipelines that automate confident decisions and route uncertain ones to human review. Such selective prediction needs a confidence score that an operator can threshold at a chosen risk level. Prior work asks whether LLM confidence is well calibrated or well ranked; we ask a complementary, deployment-oriented question that has been largely overlooked: at what resolution can the score be thresholded? We call the answer the score granularity gap. Through a controlled comparison of seven ways to build a confidence score, from a single verbalized number, to token probabilities, to querying the model many times and combining the answers, across 25 model-dataset pairs (9 LLMs, 3 benchmarks), we find that single-shot verbalized confidence, once correctly converted to a class probability, ranks cases surprisingly well, yet takes only a handful of distinct values. It therefore offers an operator only a few coarse thresholds, no matter how well it ranks. We show which constructions widen this gap, at what inference cost, and with what effect on ranking, notably that multi-query aggregation helps weak models but can degrade already-strong ones. We translate these trade-offs into concrete deployment guidance.
2606.22179.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作黑箱分類器，在自動化自信決策的流程中，並將不確定的決策交由人類審核。這種選擇性預測需要一個信心分數，操作員可以在選定的風險水平上進行閾值設定。先前的研究詢問LLM的信心是否經過良好校準或良好排序；我們提出了一個互補的、以部署為導向的問題，這個問題在很大程度上被忽視：這個分數可以在什麼解析度下進行閾值設定？我們將這個答案稱為分數粒度差距。通過對七種構建信心分數的方法進行控制比較，從單一的口頭數字到標記概率，再到多次查詢模型並結合答案，涵蓋25個模型-數據集對（9個LLMs，3個基準），我們發現單次口頭表達的信心，一旦正確轉換為類別概率，對案例的排序意外地良好，但僅取幾個不同的值。因此，無論其排序效果多麼良好，它僅為操作員提供了幾個粗略的閾值。我們展示了哪些構造擴大了這一差距，推理成本是多少，以及對排序的影響，特別是多查詢聚合有助於弱模型，但可能會降低已經強大的模型的表現。我們將這些權衡轉化為具體的部署指導。
2606.22179.author: Ao Sun et.al.
2606.22179.authors: Ao Sun, Tian Sun, Jiaxing Geng
